manifest.name: Dayflow

name: Dayflow

releases.author: caezium
releases.description:
Addresses Obsidian community store review feedback.

## Changes

- **Bundle the WASM into \`main.js\`.** The Obsidian installer only downloads \`main.js\`, \`manifest.json\`, and \`styles.css\`, so shipping the SQLite WASM as a separate release asset meant store-installed copies would fail at first sync. We now use esbuild's \`binary\` loader to inline \`sql-wasm.wasm\` directly into the bundle. \`main.js\` grew from 152 KB to ~1.0 MB but the plugin now installs cleanly via the store with no extra assets.
- **Drop \"Obsidian\" from the description.** Per the reviewer, the word is redundant in the community plugin directory.
- **Replace the \`builtin-modules\` npm package with Node's built-in \`module.builtinModules\`.** Same data, one fewer dependency.

## Install

### Via the (pending) community plugin store

After the submission is accepted, it'll appear in Settings → Community plugins → Browse → search \"Dayflow\".

### Via BRAT

\`Cmd+P → BRAT: Add a beta plugin\` → \`caezium/obsidian-dayflow-plugin\`.

### Manual

Download \`main.js\`, \`manifest.json\`, \`styles.css\` into \`<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/dayflow/\`. (No \`sql-wasm.wasm\` anymore — it's embedded.)
releases.name: 0.2.2 — community store review fixes

releases.author: caezium
releases.description:
Renames the plugin id from \`obsidian-dayflow\` to \`dayflow\` to satisfy Obsidian's [manifest guidelines](https://docs.obsidian.md/Reference/Manifest) (the word \"obsidian\" is disallowed in plugin IDs).

## Breaking change for existing manual installs

If you installed v0.2.0 manually:
1. Move the install folder: \`<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-dayflow/\` → \`<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/dayflow/\`
2. In \`<vault>/.obsidian/community-plugins.json\`, replace \`\"obsidian-dayflow\"\` with \`\"dayflow\"\`
3. Reload Obsidian

Your saved settings (\`data.json\`) move with the folder rename, so no settings loss.

If you installed via BRAT: uninstall the old plugin, then re-add \`caezium/obsidian-dayflow-plugin\` so BRAT picks up the new id.

## What's in here

Same features as v0.2.0 — see [v0.2.0 release notes](https://github.com/caezium/obsidian-dayflow-plugin/releases/tag/v0.2.0). No behavior changes.
releases.name: 0.2.1 — id rename for community store submission

releases.author: caezium
releases.description:
First public release of the Dayflow Obsidian plugin.

## Features

- **Daily notes** with `> [!info] Day at a glance` callout — covering every Dayflow table (timeline, journal, standup, goals, ratings)
- **Weekly notes** with inline SVG **treemap, bar chart, focus heatmap, and Sankey** — all theme-aware via `currentColor`
- **Side-pane Today view** that live-renders today's note with a refresh button
- **Status bar item**: `Dayflow · synced 12m ago` — click to sync
- **Bases dashboards** (one-click install): Recent days, Weekly review, Focus performance
- **ActivityWatch enrichment** (opt-in) — precise per-app minutes under every timeline card
- **Append to Obsidian daily note** — stamps a callout into your existing Daily Notes file
- **Skip days before** date bound — protects against pulling old/garbage data
- **Mobile graceful no-op** — desktop-only with runtime guard
- Triggers: on startup, every N minutes, command palette, ribbon icon, status bar click

## Privacy

- Read-only SQLite via sql.js (WASM)
- Zero network calls by default; opt-in ActivityWatch enrichment only ever talks to `localhost:5600`
- No telemetry

## Install

### Via BRAT (recommended)

1. Install [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat) from the community plugin store
2. `Cmd+P → BRAT: Add a beta plugin`
3. Paste `caezium/obsidian-dayflow-plugin`
4. Enable **Dayflow** in Settings → Community plugins

### Manual

Download the four files attached to this release into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-dayflow/`, then reload Obsidian.

## Requirements

- macOS (Dayflow.app is macOS-only)
- [Dayflow](https://dayflow.space) installed
- Obsidian 1.5.0 or newer
releases.name: v0.2.0 — first public release
